metadata.packName: Banking and Finance Microsoft 365 E5 Complex Scenarios
metadata.purpose: Provide Codex and browser-based agents with complex, multi-workload banking and finance scenarios that generate realistic Microsoft 365, Purview, Defender, Copilot, DLP, label, and insider-risk-style telemetry.

complexScenarios.title: Weekly Loan Committee Package Oversharing
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Prepare and distribute the weekly loan committee package for internal approval.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Sofia coordinates the loan committee pack using SharePoint and Teams. Carlos updates credit exposure data, Emily reviews legal caveats, and Devon is asked to upload supporting customer documents. Under time pressure, Devon shares the wrong folder link with a broad Operations channel and an external advisor mailbox.
normalActivities: Create loan committee folder in restricted SharePoint site
Apply Confidential label to committee materials
riskyActivities: Devon uploads KYC support files to a broad Operations library
Devon creates a sharing link with external access
Committee package includes fictional customer IDs and loan references
suggestedBrowserAgentFlow: Devon uploads KYC index to wrong library

complexScenarios.title: Monthly AML Review Workbook Download and External AI Upload
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Review monthly AML queue trends and prepare an internal summary.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Devon downloads the monthly AML review workbook from SharePoint to OneDrive. Instead of using approved Copilot workflows, he copies rows into an unmanaged AI tool to summarize trends. The workbook contains synthetic suspicious activity references and customer account-like identifiers.
normalActivities: Share summary with Marcus for security review
riskyActivities: Upload or paste sensitive AML data into external AI
Download regulated financial data to OneDrive
Use customer-level data instead of aggregated trends
recommendedControls: Defender for Cloud Apps session controls
DLP policy for AML and account-like values
suggestedBrowserAgentFlow: Devon opens workbook in Excel Web
Devon pastes data into external AI app

complexScenarios.title: Customer Complaint Case Shared with Internal Notes
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Respond to a high-priority customer complaint involving a disputed transaction.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: David manages a complaint case in Microsoft Lists and drafts a customer response. Devon adds internal investigation notes directly into the customer-safe document. David nearly sends the file externally before Emily flags that the attachment contains internal legal and investigation commentary.
riskyActivities: Internal notes are added to customer-facing file
Customer account-like number appears in Word comments
External email draft includes wrong attachment
recommendedControls: Trainable classifier for internal case notes
suggestedBrowserAgentFlow: Devon edits Word file and adds internal notes
David posts Teams message asking for approval

complexScenarios.title: Power BI Customer-Level Credit Export
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Prepare a weekly credit-risk summary for executive review.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Carlos reviews a Power BI credit-risk dashboard and exports underlying customer-level data to Excel for deeper analysis. Devon later forwards the export to a broad operations group instead of using the aggregated executive summary.
riskyActivities: Email export to broad distribution list
Share customer-level credit scores outside Risk Analytics

complexScenarios.title: Treasury Reconciliation Workbook Printed and Copied
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Support daily treasury reconciliation and exception review.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Devon downloads a treasury reconciliation workbook to his device, prints a copy for offline review, and then copies the workbook to a temporary network share. The workbook contains bank-like account references and reconciliation exceptions.
riskyActivities: Download to local device outside approved process
suggestedBrowserAgentFlow: Devon copies file to network share

complexScenarios.title: Regulatory Response Draft Shared Before Legal Approval
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Prepare a response package for a fictional regulator inquiry.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Emily prepares a regulator response draft in a legal privileged SharePoint site. Sofia coordinates evidence collection. Devon downloads a draft and sends it to an external advisor before legal approval, exposing internal control-gap language.

complexScenarios.title: HR Compensation Data Surfaced Through Copilot
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Prepare monthly workforce and compensation planning materials.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Laura stores HR compensation planning files in a SharePoint folder with inherited access from a broader group. Alexander asks Copilot to summarize workforce planning, and Copilot surfaces compensation details because permissions allow access. Devon also accesses the same workbook even though he has no business need.
normalActivities: Use Copilot for workforce trend summary
riskyActivities: Devon accesses HR compensation workbook outside role scope

complexScenarios.title: Customer Onboarding KYC Packet Sent to Vendor
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Complete onboarding for a fictional high-value banking customer.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: David asks Devon to prepare an internal KYC packet for review. Devon misunderstands the vendor request and sends the full KYC package externally instead of the sanitized checklist. Emily and Marcus intervene after the DLP alert triggers.
riskyActivities: Attach regulated financial data to vendor email
suggestedBrowserAgentFlow: Devon asks in Teams why it was blocked

complexScenarios.title: Suspicious Activity Review Copied to USB
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Review suspicious activity records for internal operations follow-up.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Devon accesses a suspicious activity review folder and downloads several case files. Later, endpoint telemetry shows an attempted USB copy. Security must determine whether this was a training mistake, negligence, or potential exfiltration.
riskyActivities: Mass download of suspicious activity files
Access to cases not assigned to Devon

complexScenarios.title: Vendor Due Diligence Package with Excessive Access
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Refresh monthly vendor due diligence evidence for a critical banking operations vendor.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: James coordinates vendor due diligence through a SharePoint workspace and Planner board. Devon adds an external guest to a Teams channel that contains internal vendor risk notes. Emily identifies legal concerns, and Ana reviews external access permissions.
riskyActivities: Guest user added to internal risk channel
Due diligence evidence shared with download rights

complexScenarios.title: Branch Operations Report Shared to Broad Distribution List
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Publish weekly branch operations performance metrics.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: James prepares a branch operations summary. Carlos exports metrics from Power BI. Devon forwards the raw export to a broad internal distribution list, including customer complaints and branch-level exception counts.
riskyActivities: Forward raw export to broad distribution list
Include branch exception details and customer complaint IDs
Use attachment instead of controlled SharePoint link
recommendedControls: Use SharePoint links instead of attachments

complexScenarios.title: Loan Exception Tracker Exposed Through Microsoft List Export
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Track missing or incomplete loan documentation exceptions.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: David maintains a Microsoft List for loan documentation exceptions. Devon exports the list to Excel to filter cases, then emails the export to a partner mailbox. The export includes loan IDs, customer IDs, and exception notes.
recommendedControls: Restrict external sharing of regulated financial data

complexScenarios.title: Finance Close Evidence Collection and Label Downgrade
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Prepare monthly finance close evidence package for leadership review.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Carlos prepares finance close evidence and applies a Confidential label. Devon creates a derivative copy and downgrades the label to Internal to send it to a broader audience. The file still contains account-like and treasury references.
riskyActivities: Send finance evidence to broad audience

complexScenarios.title: Collections Case Notes in Teams Chat
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Coordinate collections follow-up for fictional delinquent accounts.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: David asks the team for help with collections cases. Devon pastes customer account-like numbers and hardship notes directly into a broad Teams channel. Marcus receives a compliance signal and reminds the team to use the restricted case workspace.
riskyActivities: Include hardship notes in broad channel
Discuss sensitive customer circumstances outside restricted workspace
suggestedBrowserAgentFlow: David moves discussion to restricted channel

complexScenarios.title: Executive Board Deck Built from Sensitive Source Files
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Create monthly board reporting deck using finance, risk, and operations inputs.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Alexander asks Copilot to draft the board deck using multiple source files. The generated draft includes customer-level risk details from Carlos and model predictions from Priya. Sofia must sanitize the final board deck before publication.
riskyActivities: Source files have mixed sensitivity levels
suggestedBrowserAgentFlow: Final PDF is uploaded to board site

complexScenarios.title: Fraud Investigation Whiteboard Exported Externally
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Map the timeline of a fictional fraud investigation and coordinate next steps.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Marcus creates a Whiteboard during a fraud investigation meeting. Devon exports the whiteboard as a PDF and sends it to an external advisor. The whiteboard includes internal control gaps, account-like references, and investigation hypotheses.

complexScenarios.title: Security Awareness Example Accidentally Uses Realistic Sensitive Patterns
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Publish monthly security awareness content about data handling.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Marcus prepares awareness material using synthetic examples. Devon contributes examples copied from a restricted banking workbook. The draft post includes account-like numbers and customer identifiers, triggering a DLP review before publication.
riskyActivities: Use realistic sensitive patterns in broad awareness post
Post sensitive-looking values to Viva Engage

complexScenarios.title: Meeting Recording Transcript Contains Customer Data
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Run weekly customer operations review meeting and publish recording internally.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: During the customer operations review, Devon reads customer account-like identifiers aloud while describing a dispute case. The Teams recording transcript captures the sensitive details and is later shared broadly in SharePoint.
riskyActivities: Transcript is downloaded by unauthorized users

complexScenarios.title: External Consultant Added to Risk Committee Team
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Coordinate temporary external consulting support for risk committee preparation.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Sofia adds an external consultant to a Risk Committee Team for schedule coordination. The Team contains historical loan committee files, AML notes, and board prep materials. Devon shares a sensitive file into the same channel after the guest is added.
normalActivities: Add external consultant to limited collaboration space
riskyActivities: Guest access to sensitive historical files
Sensitive file posted after guest joins
suggestedBrowserAgentFlow: Ana removes guest and creates restricted channel

complexScenarios.title: Forms-Based Customer Complaint Intake Captures Sensitive Data
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Collect internal customer complaint intake details for operations triage.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: David creates an internal complaint intake form. Devon changes the form settings and makes it accessible outside the organization. Respondents enter account-like identifiers and transaction references in free-text fields.

complexScenarios.title: Privileged Legal Memo Copied into Loop Component
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Coordinate legal and operational actions after a fictional regulatory inquiry.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Emily drafts a privileged legal memo. During a Teams meeting, Devon copies a paragraph into a Loop component used for PMO action tracking. The Loop component is visible to non-legal project members.
riskyActivities: Privileged content copied into broadly visible Loop component
Legal analysis exposed to non-legal users
Copilot may summarize Loop content later

complexScenarios.title: Unusual Sign-In Followed by Sensitive File Access
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Investigate unusual access to sensitive banking operations content.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Devon signs in from an unusual location and then accesses several regulated financial files in SharePoint. Conditional Access challenges the session, and Defender correlates unusual location with sensitive file downloads.
normalActivities: User signs in from usual work location
riskyActivities: Sensitive file access after risky sign-in

complexScenarios.title: Customer Risk Model Feature Matrix Shared with Data Vendor
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Validate customer risk model features with an external analytics vendor.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Priya prepares an anonymized feature matrix for vendor review. Devon attaches the pre-anonymized version to an external email because the file names are similar. The raw file includes customer IDs, account-like values, and model risk scores.

complexScenarios.title: Executive Assistant Sends Board Appendix to Wrong Audience
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Distribute board materials to approved executive recipients.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: The board appendix includes customer concentration, credit exposure, and strategic margin assumptions. A distribution mistake sends the appendix to a broader leadership list. Ana reviews the DLP and access signals and recommends link revocation and label enforcement.
normalActivities: Send approved link to board recipients
riskyActivities: Send board appendix to broad leadership list
Use attachment instead of restricted link
recommendedControls: Block attachment of Highly Confidential files
suggestedBrowserAgentFlow: Sofia recalls message and sends restricted link

complexScenarios.title: Audit Evidence Package Downloaded After Role Change Notice
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Investigate sensitive audit evidence access after an HR role-change signal.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: After a fictional role-change notice, Devon downloads audit evidence packages, copies one file to OneDrive, and prints a regulatory evidence index. The behavior creates an insider-risk-style sequence requiring HR, Legal, and Security coordination.
riskyActivities: Download sensitive audit evidence after HR event
Copy files to personal OneDrive folder

complexScenarios.title: Customer Support Email Includes Transaction Monitoring Details
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Respond to a customer inquiry about a delayed transaction review.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: David drafts a customer email about a delayed transaction review. Devon suggests including internal transaction monitoring reasons. Emily warns that the explanation exposes internal monitoring logic and should be replaced with a customer-safe response.

complexScenarios.title: M&A Banking Strategy Workspace Overexposed
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Prepare confidential banking strategy and acquisition readiness materials.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Alexander and Emily work on a fictional acquisition strategy. Carlos provides financial exposure data. The workspace is accidentally created with broad leadership access. Devon accesses the folder and asks Copilot to summarize acquisition assumptions.
riskyActivities: Copilot summarizes acquisition assumptions for unauthorized user
suggestedBrowserAgentFlow: Ana reviews permissions and Copilot exposure

complexScenarios.title: Policy Exception Request with Sensitive Attachments
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Review a policy exception request for temporary access to a restricted banking operations repository.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Miguel creates a policy exception intake process. Devon submits a request and attaches a customer operations export as justification. The attachment contains regulated data and should not be part of an access request form.
normalActivities: Approve or reject based on business need
riskyActivities: Attach sensitive customer export to access request
Use broad request workflow for regulated data
recommendedControls: DLP for Forms exports and attachments
suggestedBrowserAgentFlow: Ana rejects request and asks for business justification

complexScenarios.title: Sanitized Dataset Replaced by Raw Dataset Before Demo
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Prepare a customer-facing banking analytics demo using sanitized data only.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Priya prepares a sanitized dataset for Diego's customer demo. Devon replaces the dataset in the demo folder with a raw file that includes customer IDs and account-like values. Diego uses the folder link in a customer email without noticing the replacement.
suggestedBrowserAgentFlow: Devon uploads raw dataset with similar name

complexScenarios.title: End-to-End Devon Risk Chain Across Banking Operations
complexScenarios.businessObjective: Create a multi-day ambiguous insider-risk-style narrative combining normal work, mistakes, unsafe AI use, external sharing, endpoint movement, and investigation response.
complexScenarios.businessNarrative: Over several days, Devon performs a sequence of activities that individually could be explained as operational pressure but collectively indicate elevated risk. He downloads KYC, AML, and customer complaint files; asks Copilot to summarize restricted data; pastes sensitive rows into an external AI tool; shares one file externally; prints a treasury workbook; and copies a suspicious activity review file to a network share after a role-change conversation.
recommendedControls: DLP across Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams
suggestedBrowserAgentFlow: Day 1: Devon accesses assigned customer operations files
Day 1: Devon downloads KYC and complaint files
Day 2: Devon asks Copilot to summarize restricted files
Day 2: Devon pastes rows into external AI app
Day 3: Devon emails a file externally and overrides warning
Day 4: Devon copies SAR file to network share
Day 4: Marcus and Ana open insider risk investigation
Day 5: Emily and Laura review appropriate response
Day 5: Access is remediated and coaching outcome is documented
